text: Most teams do not need a bigger AI model first. They need a reliable workflow: intake, enrichment, routing, calendar handoff, and a human fallback. I mapped the n8n workflow stack I would use for that here: http://localhost:4174/comparisons/best-n8n-workflows-for-lead-routing.html

OpenClaw is interesting for technical agent experiments, but most operators asking about it need something deployable this week. I built the plain-English comparison against n8n and adjacent automation tools: http://localhost:4174/comparisons/openclaw-vs-n8n.html

Restaurant AI has a brutally simple ROI story: missed calls, slow reservations, order mistakes, and staff interruptions. I compared the phone-agent options I would evaluate first: http://localhost:4174/comparisons/best-restaurant-ai-phone-agents.html
New comparison live: n8n workflows for lead routing. Intake, enrichment, scheduling, and follow-up without turning every handoff into spreadsheet glue.
Restaurant AI tools worth watching: phone agents for missed calls, QR menu software, and AI food photos. The practical stack is smaller than the hype.
OpenClaw vs n8n is really agent runtime vs workflow operations. I wrote the operator-focused comparison for people choosing what to deploy.
